_meta.title: Singapore property decision — regulatory & loan-engine constants
_meta.not_advice: Educational illustration of published rules, not financial or mortgage advice. Verify against MAS / MoneySense / IRAS / HDB before acting.
_meta.stability_note: Buyer-side parameters (TDSR, MSR, LTV, BSD, ABSD) unchanged since 27 Apr 2023 (ABSD/BSD) and 20 Aug 2024 (HDB LTV). Seller-side is NOT static: SSD holding period extended 3y->4y with rates raised 4pp on 4 Jul 2025 (encoded below); EC-specific tightening 8 May 2026 (see market.json ec_policy_2026).
_meta.sources: MAS; MoneySense (retrieved Jul 2026); IRAS / MOF (15 Feb 2023 stamp-duty revision); HDB / MND (20 Aug 2024).

tdsr.note: Total monthly debt obligations must not exceed 55% of gross monthly income. The property instalment in the test is computed at the higher of the 4.0% p.a. medium-term floor or the actual rate. Binding constraint for most private-property buyers.
tdsr.source: MAS (medium-term rate effective 30 Sep 2022; 55% TDSR since 16 Dec 2021)

msr.note: Mortgage Servicing Ratio: housing loan instalment must not exceed 30% of gross monthly income. Applies ON TOP of TDSR for HDB and new ECs; does not apply to private condos or resale ECs past MOP.

ltv.note: Loan-to-value ceilings for bank loans to individuals. The MAX tier applies only if BOTH: loan tenure within the cliff AND borrower age + tenure <= 65 at the end of the loan. Otherwise the REDUCED tier applies and the minimum cash portion rises.

reduction_trigger.note: If tenure exceeds 30y (private) / 25y (HDB) OR the loan runs past age 65, LTV steps down to the reduced tier and first-loan minimum cash rises from 5% to 10%.

hdb_concessionary.note: HDB loan LTV cut 80%->75% on 20 Aug 2024. Downpayment can be fully CPF (no minimum cash). Tenure capped at the shorter of 25y and age 65. Eligible loan sized at a 3% floor (introduced 30 Sep 2022). Household income ceiling S$14,000 (S$7,000 singles).
hdb_concessionary.source: HDB/MND (20 Aug 2024); MAS/MND/HDB joint release (29 Sep 2022); HDB HLE

tenure_cap_years.note: Absolute maximum loan tenures. Separate from the LTV cliff at 30y/25y/age-65 above. For a borrower of age A wanting full 75% LTV on a private purchase, usable tenure = min(30, 65 - A). An EC bought from a developer follows the private 35y cap: Notice 632 para 22 applies the 30y cap to 'HDB Flat' as defined via the Housing and Development Act, and para 8(a)(i) lists ECs separately from HDB Flats - so ECs fall under the para 21 general 35y cap.
tenure_cap_years.flag: Many banks nonetheless cap EC tenure at 30y in practice; beyond 30y the reduced LTV tier applies in any case.
tenure_cap_years.source: MAS Notice 632 (last revised 21 Aug 2025), paras 8(a)(i), 21, 22 and the HDB Flat definition; MAS/MoneySense explainers (Jul 2026)

absd.note: Additional Buyer's Stamp Duty, on the higher of price or market value. Index = [1st property, 2nd, 3rd or more]. US nationals, and nationals/PRs of Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway and Switzerland, are treated as Singapore Citizens under FTAs. Unchanged since 27 Apr 2023.

bsd.note: Buyer's Stamp Duty, residential, effective 15 Feb 2023, on the higher of price or market value. Marginal tiers.

ssd.note: Seller's Stamp Duty, residential: payable if the property is sold within 4 years of purchase, on the higher of price or market value, by year of sale (year 1 = 16%). Applies to purchases on/after 4 Jul 2025; earlier purchases keep the prior 3-year 12/8/4% schedule. HDB's 5-year MOP normally outlasts it.
ssd.source: MAS/MOF/MND joint release 3 Jul 2025; IRAS SSD (residential)

sora_3m.flag: published by MAS; retrieved via an aggregator, not independently verified to the basis point

sora_history.cycle: near 0.2% in early 2022, above 3% by end-2022, around 3.5% through 2023-2024, then down to about 1% by mid-2026
sora_history.source: MAS-published 3M compounded SORA, via aggregators (moneylobang; MoneySmart), cross-checked to the curated spot
sora_history.flag: curated month-end readings, indicative to ~5bp; MAS publishes the authoritative daily series

rates.note: Live 3M SORA is scrapeable from the MAS domestic-interest-rates page; package rates have no public feed and are curated here. Refresh at each review.

cpf.note: CPF Ordinary Account pays 2.5% p.a. Cash used for downpayment/instalments has an opportunity cost at least equal to this (and typically the investor's portfolio return). Used in the cash-vs-mortgage module as the CPF hurdle.
